title: Intro to alphaN
text: install latest release version CRAN use: can install development version alphaN GitHub :

title: Intro to alphaN
text: vignette provides introduction basic functionality alphaN. full details methodology, please refer Wulff & Taylor (2024).

title: Intro to alphaN
text: Using alphaN function, can get alpha level need use obtain desired level evidence testing regression coefficient regression model. example: planning run linear regression model 1000 observations. thus set n = 1000. default BF 1 meaning want avoid Lindley’s paradox, .e. just want null alternative least equally likely reject null. Therefore, obtain evidence least 1, set alpha 0.0086.

what: Plotting the relationship between the Bayes factor and p-value
title: Intro to alphaN
text: alphaN function works mapping p-value Bayes factor. relationship can shown using JAB_plot. instance:  alpha level needed achieve Bayes factor 1 shown red triangle plot. Lines achieving Bayes factors 3 (moderate evidence) 10 (strong evidence) also shown default. evident lower alpha level needed achieve higher evidence.

what: Alpha as a decreasing function of N
title: Intro to alphaN
text: important point procedure alpha set function sample size. larger sample size, lower alpha needed significant result can interpreted evidence alternative. graph illustrates relationship previous example:
code: seqN <- seq(50, 1000, 1) plot(seqN, alphaN(seqN), type = "l",      xlab = "n", ylab = "Alpha")

title: Intro to alphaN
text: set alpha level function sample size, need choose prior carefully. alphaN allows user choose four sensible prior options based suggestions previous literature: Jeffreys’ approximate BF (method = "JAB"), minimal training sample (method = "min"), robust minimal training sample (method = "robust"), balanced Type-Type-II errors (method = "balanced"). method = "JAB" good choice users want conservative small effects, method = "min" MLE misspecified, method = "robust" MLE misspecified sample size small, method = "balanced" Type-II errors costly. instance, achieve evidence 3 1,000 observations ensure balanced error rates, run package contains convenience function alphaN_plot allows quick comparison alpha function sample size four different methods:

title: Authors and Citation
text: Jesper Wulff. Author, maintainer. Luke Taylor. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Wulff, J. N., & Taylor, L. (2024). alpha depend sample size: Bayesian-frequentist compromise significance testing. Strategic Organization. doi:10.1177/14761270231214429
code: @Article{,   title = {How and why alpha should depend on sample size: A Bayesian-frequentist compromise for significance testing},   author = {Jesper Wulff and Luke Taylor},   journal = {Strategic Organization},   year = {2024},   doi = {10.1177/14761270231214429}, }

what: Set Alpha Based on Sample Size Using Bayes Factors
title: Set Alpha Based on Sample Size Using Bayes Factors
text: goal alphaN help user set significance level function sample size. function alphaN allows users set significance level function sample size based evidence prior features desire. function JABt JABp converts test statistics pp-values sample size dependent Bayes factors. JAB_plot plots Bayes factor function pp-value, alphaN_plot plots alpha level function sample size given Bayes factor. Calculations based Wulff & Taylor (2024). enjoy package, please consider citing paper (see citation("alphaN")). ’re R user, may also interested associated Shiny app.

title: Set Alpha Based on Sample Size Using Bayes Factors
text: install latest release version CRAN use: can install development version alphaN GitHub :

title: Set Alpha Based on Sample Size Using Bayes Factors
text: example: planning run linear regression model 1000 observations. thus set n = 1000. default BF 1 meaning want avoid Lindley’s paradox, .e., just want null alternative least equally likely reject null. Therefore, obtain evidence least 1, set alpha 0.0086.

what: Transforms a t-statistic from a glm or lm object into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JAB
title: Transforms a t-statistic from a glm or lm object into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JAB
text: Extracts test statistic coefficient fitted model object converts Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor, given sample size used fit.

title: Transforms a t-statistic from a glm or lm object into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JAB
Transforms a t-statistic from a glm or lm object into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JAB
text: glm_obj glm lm object. covariate name covariate want BF string. method Used choice 'b'. Currently one : "JAB": choice b produces Jeffreys' approximate BF (Wagenmakers, 2022) "min": uses minimal training sample prior (Gu et al., 2018) "robust": robust version "min" prevents small b (O'Hagan, 1995) "balanced": choice b balances type type II errors (Gu et al., 2016) upper upper limit range realistic effect sizes. relevant method="balanced". Defaults 1 range realistic effect sizes uniformly distributed 0 1, U(0,1).

title: Transforms a t-statistic from a glm or lm object into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JAB
text: numeric value BF favour H1.

title: Transforms a t-statistic from a glm or lm object into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JAB
code: # Simulate data  ## Sample size n <- 200  ## Regressors Z1 <- runif(n, -1, 1) Z2 <- runif(n, -1, 1) Z3 <- runif(n, -1, 1) Z4 <- runif(n, -1, 1) X <- runif(n, -1, 1)  ## Error term U <- rnorm(n, 0, 0.5)  ## Outcome Y <- X/sqrt(n) + U  # Run a GLM LM <- glm(Y ~ X + Z1 + Z2 + Z3 + Z4)  # Compute JAB for "X" based on the regression results JAB(LM, "X") #> [1] 0.07981323  # Compute JAB using the minimum prior JAB(LM, "X", method = "min") #> [1] 0.1128729

title: Plots JAB as a function of the p-value — JAB_plot
text: Plots JAB function p-value

title: Plots JAB as a function of the p-value — JAB_plot
Plots JAB as a function of the p-value — JAB_plot
text: n Sample size. positive numeric vector. BF Bayes factor like match. 1 avoid Lindley's Paradox, 3 achieve moderate evidence 10 achieve strong evidence. method Used choice 'b'. Currently one : "JAB": choice b produces Jeffreys' approximate BF (Wagenmakers, 2022) "min": uses minimal training sample prior (Gu et al., 2018) "robust": robust version "min" prevents small b (O'Hagan, 1995) "balanced": choice b balances type type II errors (Gu et al., 2016) upper upper limit range realistic effect sizes. relevant method="balanced". Defaults 1 range realistic effect sizes uniformly distributed 0 1, U(0,1).

title: Plots JAB as a function of the p-value — JAB_plot
text: Prints plot.

title: Plots JAB as a function of the p-value — JAB_plot
code: # Plot JAB as function of the p-value for a sample size of 2000 JAB_plot(2000)

what: Transforms a p-value into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JABp
title: Transforms a p-value into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JABp
text: Converts two-sided p-value z- t-test Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor, given sample size.

title: Transforms a p-value into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JABp
code: JABp(n, p, z = TRUE, df = NULL, method = "JAB", upper = 1)

title: Transforms a p-value into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JABp
text: n Sample size. positive numeric vector. p two-sided p-value. z p-value based z- t-statistic? TRUE z. df z=FALSE, provide degrees freedom t-statistic. method Used choice 'b'. Currently one : "JAB": choice b produces Jeffreys' approximate BF (Wagenmakers, 2022) "min": uses minimal training sample prior (Gu et al., 2018) "robust": robust version "min" prevents small b (O'Hagan, 1995) "balanced": choice b balances type type II errors (Gu et al., 2016) upper upper limit range realistic effect sizes. relevant method="balanced". Defaults 1 range realistic effect sizes uniformly distributed 0 1, U(0,1).

title: Transforms a p-value into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JABp
text: numeric value BF favour H1.

title: Transforms a p-value into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JABp
code: # Transform a p-value of 0.007038863 from a z-test into JAB # using a sample size of 200. JABp(200, 0.007038863) #> [1] 2.670735  # Transform a p-value of 0.007038863 from a t-test with 190 # degrees of freedom into JAB using a sample size of 200. JABp(200, 0.007038863, z=FALSE, df=190) #> [1] 2.893791

Transforms a t-statistic into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JABt
text: n Sample size. positive numeric vector. t t-statistic. method Used choice 'b'. Currently one : "JAB": choice b produces Jeffreys' approximate BF (Wagenmakers, 2022) "min": uses minimal training sample prior (Gu et al., 2018) "robust": robust version "min" prevents small b (O'Hagan, 1995) "balanced": choice b balances type type II errors (Gu et al., 2016) upper upper limit range realistic effect sizes. relevant method="balanced". Defaults 1 range realistic effect sizes uniformly distributed 0 1, U(0,1).

title: Transforms a t-statistic into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JABt
text: numeric value BF favour H1.

title: Transforms a t-statistic into Jeffreys' approximate Bayes factor — JABt
code: # Transform a t-statistic of 2.695 computed based on a sample size of 200 into JAB JABt(200, 2.695) #> [1] 2.670735

what: Set the alpha level based on sample size for coefficients in a regression model — alphaN
title: Set the alpha level based on sample size for coefficients in a regression model — alphaN
text: Computes alpha level required achieve desired level evidence, expressed Bayes factor, testing coefficient regression model. alpha level decreasing function sample size. Vectorized n BF.

title: Set the alpha level based on sample size for coefficients in a regression model — alphaN
Set the alpha level based on sample size for coefficients in a regression model — alphaN
text: n Sample size. positive numeric vector. BF Bayes factor like match. 1 avoid Lindley's Paradox, 3 achieve moderate evidence 10 achieve strong evidence. method Used choice 'b'. Currently one : "JAB": choice b produces Jeffreys' approximate BF (Wagenmakers, 2022) "min": uses minimal training sample prior (Gu et al., 2018) "robust": robust version "min" prevents small b (O'Hagan, 1995) "balanced": choice b balances type type II errors (Gu et al., 2016) upper upper limit range realistic effect sizes. relevant method="balanced". Defaults 1 range realistic effect sizes uniformly distributed 0 1, U(0,1).

title: Set the alpha level based on sample size for coefficients in a regression model — alphaN
text: Numeric alpha level required achieve desired level evidence.

title: Set the alpha level based on sample size for coefficients in a regression model — alphaN
text: Gu et al. (2016). Error probabilities default Bayesian hypothesis testing. Journal Mathematical Psychology, 72, 130–143.  Gu et al. (2018). Approximated adjusted fractional Bayes factors: general method testing informative hypotheses. British Journal Mathematical Statistical Psychology, 71(2).  O’Hagan, . (1995). Fractional Bayes Factors Model Comparison. Journal Royal Statistical Society. Series B (Methodological), 57(1), 99–138.  Wagenmakers, E.-J. (2022). Approximate objective Bayes factors p-values sample size: 3p(sqrt(n)) rule. PsyArXiv.  Wulff, J. N., & Taylor, L. (2024). alpha depend sample size: Bayesian-frequentist compromise significance testing. Strategic Organization. doi:10.1177/14761270231214429

title: Set the alpha level based on sample size for coefficients in a regression model — alphaN
code: # Plot of alpha level as a function of n seqN <- seq(50, 1000, 1) plot(seqN, alphaN(seqN), type = "l")

what: Creates a plot of alpha as function of sample size for each of the four prior options — alphaN_plot

text: Creates plot alpha function sample size four prior options

title: Creates a plot of alpha as function of sample size for each of the four prior options — alphaN_plot
Creates a plot of alpha as function of sample size for each of the four prior options — alphaN_plot
text: BF Bayes factor like match. 1 avoid Lindley's Paradox, 3 achieve moderate evidence 10 achieve strong evidence. max maximum number sample size. Defaults 10,000. ylim Limits y-axis. default, NULL, covers four curves. Set e.g. c(0, 0.05) zoom small alpha levels.

title: Creates a plot of alpha as function of sample size for each of the four prior options — alphaN_plot
text: Prints plot.

title: Creates a plot of alpha as function of sample size for each of the four prior options — alphaN_plot
code: # Plot of alpha level as a function of n for a Bayes factor of 3 alphaN_plot(BF = 3)

title: alphaN 0.1.3
text: alphaN() JABt() now return correct results n vector method = "robust" method = "balanced". Previously, "robust" silently applied smallest sample size every element "balanced" failed unrelated error.

title: alphaN 0.1.3
text: functions now validate inputs fail informative error messages: mistyped method, missing df JABp(..., z = FALSE), p outside (0, 1], non-positive n BF, unknown covariate JAB() (now lists coefficients available model). JAB_plot() gained upper argument, passed underlying computations method = "balanced". alphaN_plot() gained ylim argument. default now covers four curves; previously y-axis fixed (0, 0.05), silently clipped "balanced" curve small Bayes factors. JAB() now determines sample size via nobs().

title: alphaN 0.1.3
text: ?JABp longer placeholder title. Corrected Wagenmakers (2022) reference (year, title) updated Wulff & Taylor reference published version (2024, Strategic Organization, doi:10.1177/14761270231214429) documentation, README, vignette. Added CITATION file companion paper. Fixed typos vignette documented JABp() expects two-sided p-value.
